admission_criteria: Liveborn infant delivered in-hospital, gestational age >=35 0/7 wks, birth weight typically >=2000-2500g, physiologically stable transitioning newborn assigned to well-newborn/mother-baby couplet (rooming-in) care; requires no resuscitation beyond routine drying/stimulation; APGAR >=7 at 5 min and no supplemental O2 requirement. NOT DRG 795 (route to NICU/special care): persistent respiratory distress, GA <35 wks, major congenital anomaly, sustained hypoglycemia/hypothermia, or clinical sepsis. The birth admission is the index inpatient stay (no ED-discharge/observation alternative for the healthy newborn).
risk_stratification: APGAR at 1 and 5 min (>=7 normal; if <7 at 5 min repeat q5min to 20 min); Neonatal Early-Onset Sepsis (EOS) risk calculator (Kaiser/Puopolo, for >=34-35 wks) using GBS status, intrapartum antibiotics, max maternal temp, ROM duration, GA -> EOS risk per 1000 driving routine care vs enhanced observation vs blood culture +/- empiric antibiotics; AAP 2022 hyperbilirubinemia hour-specific nomogram plus neurotoxicity risk factors (isoimmune hemolytic disease, G6PD deficiency, sepsis, albumin <3.0) -> GA/risk-specific phototherapy and escalation thresholds; protocolized hypoglycemia screening (AAP/Adamkin) for at-risk (LGA, SGA, IDM, late-preterm) treating glucose <40-45 mg/dL; Ballard gestational-age assessment; NEWT weight-loss nomogram (loss >75th percentile flags feeding failure).
initial_workup_labs: Infant/cord blood type, Rh, and direct antiglobulin test (DAT/Coombs) if mother O and/or Rh-negative or antibody positive; point-of-care glucose per hypoglycemia protocol in at-risk infants (first check ~30-60 min after first feed); universal predischarge total serum or transcutaneous bilirubin at 24-48h; CBC with differential, blood culture, and inflammatory markers ONLY when EOS risk is elevated; review maternal serologies (HBsAg, HIV, RPR/syphilis, rubella, GBS); state newborn metabolic/genetic screen (dried blood spot) drawn at 24-48h once feeding established.
initial_imaging_dx: Routine normal newborn requires minimal imaging; universal CCHD pulse-oximetry screen at >=24h of age (pre-ductal right hand + post-ductal foot; pass = SpO2 >=95% in both and <=3% difference; fail = <90% anywhere); universal newborn hearing screen (OAE or automated ABR) before discharge per EHDI 1-3-6; echocardiogram/pediatric cardiology only if CCHD screen fails or pathologic murmur; hip exam (Ortolani/Barlow) with targeted hip ultrasound at 4-6 wks only if risk factors (breech, positive family history).
time_critical_interventions: Delayed cord clamping >=30-60 sec for the vigorous infant (ACOG/AAP); immediate skin-to-skin with thermoregulation (axillary temp 36.5-37.5C); initiate breastfeeding within the first hour; IM vitamin K 0.5-1 mg within 6h to prevent VKDB (do not defer/omit); erythromycin 0.5% ophthalmic ointment within 24h; hepatitis B birth-dose vaccine within 24h for stable infants >=2000g; if mother HBsAg-positive or unknown, give HBV vaccine + HBIG within 12h; first glucose check within 30-60 min of first feed in at-risk infants.
initial_management: Physiologic/supportive care: rooming-in, on-demand breastfeeding 8-12x/24h with lactation support (or iron-fortified formula), thermoneutral environment; prophylaxis (vitamin K IM, erythromycin eye ointment, HBV vaccine) as above; hypoglycemia protocol = feed then recheck, buccal 40% dextrose gel 200 mg/kg for asymptomatic transitional hypoglycemia to avoid NICU transfer, IV D10 2 mL/kg bolus if symptomatic or refractory; phototherapy when TSB at/above the AAP 2022 GA- and risk-specific threshold; escalation-of-care when TSB within 2 mg/dL of the exchange-transfusion threshold; NO routine antibiotics (reserve for EOS-calculator-indicated cases).
day1_milestones: DOL0 (birth day): complete delivery-room care and prophylaxis (vitamin K, eye ointment, HBV); confirm successful transition (stable vitals, temp, color, RR <60 without distress); establish feeding with latch assessment; document first void (expect <24h) and first meconium stool (expect <48h); record birth weight as baseline; initiate glucose protocol if at-risk; assign EOS risk category and act on it.
day2_milestones: DOL1: ongoing feeding-effectiveness assessment (breastfeeding transfer, increasing voids/stools); daily weight tracking percent loss vs NEWT (flag >7-10%); obtain predischarge bilirubin (TcB/TSB) at 24-48h and plot on the AAP 2022 nomogram -> phototherapy decision plus risk-based follow-up timing; draw newborn metabolic screen after 24h of feeding; perform CCHD pulse-ox screen at >=24h; complete newborn hearing screen; jaundice and weight check.
day3plus_milestones: DOL2+: confirm all universal screens complete and documented (metabolic, CCHD, hearing) with results/plan; reassess bilirubin trajectory and set the specific outpatient recheck interval; ensure feeding established with acceptable weight-loss trend and adequate output; complete elective circumcision with pain control plus post-procedure bleeding check; verify all discharge criteria met and follow-up scheduled; for early discharge (<48h) mandate a 48-72h PCP visit.
consults_multidisciplinary: Lactation/IBCLC for all breastfeeding dyads (early, especially if difficulty); audiology for hearing-screen fails; social work + case management for psychosocial risk (substance exposure, teen/single parent, housing instability, prior CPS); pediatric cardiology if CCHD screen fails; neonatology/NICU for instability; pharmacy for maternal-medication/lactation compatibility and NAS management if opioid-exposed; nutrition if feeding/weight concerns; interpreter/child-life services as needed; confirm a primary-care medical home is identified before discharge.
monitoring_level: Well-newborn nursery / mother-baby couplet (rooming-in) with routine vitals (temp, HR, RR, color, tone) each shift after transition, plus close observation during the first ~2h transition period; escalate to special care/NICU for persistent respiratory distress (RR >60, grunting, retractions, sustained SpO2 <90%), temperature instability, recurrent or symptomatic hypoglycemia, poor perfusion, apnea, or clinical EOS signs; TSB approaching the exchange threshold triggers escalation-of-care (intensive phototherapy, urgent labs, possible transfer).
nutrition_mobility_vte: Exclusive breastfeeding on demand (8-12 feeds/24h) is the default per AAP 2022 (support skin-to-skin, rooming-in, avoid unnecessary supplementation); iron-fortified formula if not breastfeeding or medically indicated; VTE prophylaxis is NOT applicable to newborns; glucose homeostasis maintained by early/frequent feeding plus protocolized dextrose gel; no indwelling lines/catheters in the normal newborn (umbilical lines only in NICU); model safe-sleep (supine, alone, firm bare crib) from admission.
discharge_criteria: AAP criteria: stable vitals >=12h (RR <60, HR 100-190, axillary temp 36.5-37.4C in an open crib); >=2 successful feeds with coordinated suck/swallow documented; >=1 void and >=1-2 stools; no excessive bleeding at circumcision for >=2h; predischarge bilirubin measured with a risk-stratified follow-up plan; no significant jaundice before 24h; EOS risk resolved/observed; metabolic, CCHD, and hearing screens completed; HBV vaccine given or planned; maternal/family education completed; PCP follow-up arranged; social/environmental risks addressed; minimum stay per NMHPA (~48h vaginal / ~96h cesarean) unless all early-discharge criteria fully met.
discharge_planning: Home disposition with an identified medical home; medication reconciliation (vitamin D 400 IU/day for breastfed infants, HBV documented, maternal-medication lactation review); schedule PCP visit within 48-72h (mandatory if discharged <48h) for weight, jaundice, and feeding recheck; teach return precautions (jaundice, poor feeding, lethargy, fever, decreased output, labored breathing); car-seat use plus car-seat tolerance screening for late-preterm/low-birth-weight; safe-sleep ABCs, breastfeeding support/resources, smoke-free home, and shaken-baby/abusive-head-trauma prevention; ensure newborn-screen, hearing, and CCHD result tracking/handoff to the PCP.
expected_los: Approximately 2 days typical: ~48h after uncomplicated vaginal delivery, ~3-4 days when the newborn rooms-in with a post-cesarean mother; CMS MS-DRG 795 GMLOS is approximately 2.5-3.0 days (approximate; low relative weight ~0.16-0.20).
target_los: 2 days (~48h) for the vaginal-delivery dyad; ~3 days when linked to a cesarean; earlier discharge (24-48h) acceptable ONLY if all AAP discharge criteria are met and a guaranteed 48-72h follow-up is in place.
quality_metrics: Joint Commission Perinatal Care PC-05 exclusive breast-milk feeding rate; hepatitis B birth-dose immunization coverage; newborn metabolic-screen completion and timeliness; CCHD pulse-oximetry screening rate; newborn hearing-screening rate (EHDI 1-3-6, screen by 1 month); predischarge bilirubin screening rate; 7- and 30-day newborn readmission (esp. hyperbilirubinemia/dehydration); breastfeeding-at-discharge rate; avoidance of unnecessary NICU admission and antibiotic exposure; timely (48-72h) PCP follow-up completion.
common_complications: Hyperbilirubinemia/jaundice (most common; mitigated by universal predischarge bilirubin + nomogram-guided phototherapy/follow-up); transitional or at-risk hypoglycemia (protocolized screening + dextrose gel to avoid NICU transfer); suboptimal breastfeeding with excess weight loss and dehydration (lactation support, output/weight tracking, NEWT); early-onset sepsis (EOS-calculator-guided to avoid over-treatment); hypothermia (skin-to-skin, thermoregulation); undetected CCHD or hearing loss (universal screening).
readmission_drivers: #1 = hyperbilirubinemia requiring readmission/phototherapy (lever: predischarge TSB plus risk-based 24-72h recheck; never discharge too early without follow-up); dehydration/hypernatremia from inadequate breastfeeding intake (lever: lactation support, weight/output monitoring, early weight-check visit); feeding difficulty/failure to thrive; missed or late newborn-screen/CCHD detection; late-preterm (35-36 wk) infants disproportionately bounce back (use a lower discharge threshold and closer follow-up).
guideline_source: American Academy of Pediatrics (AAP). Primary: AAP 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline, Management of Hyperbilirubinemia in the Newborn Infant >=35 Weeks (Kemper et al., Pediatrics 2022); AAP Committee on Fetus and Newborn, Hospital Stay for Healthy Term Newborn Infants (Pediatrics 2015, reaffirmed); AAP/COFN 2018 Management of Neonates >=35 Weeks With Suspected/Proven Early-Onset Sepsis (Puopolo); AAP 2011 Postnatal Glucose Homeostasis (Adamkin); AAP 2022 Breastfeeding and the Use of Human Milk (Meek); plus ACOG/AAP delayed cord clamping and state newborn-screening/EHDI/CCHD mandates.
key_citations: Per PubMed: Kemper AR et al. Clinical Practice Guideline Revision: Management of Hyperbilirubinemia in the Newborn Infant 35 or More Weeks of Gestation. Pediatrics 2022;150(3):e2022058859 (PMID 35927462; DOI 10.1542/peds.2022-058859); Slaughter JL, Kemper AR, Newman TB. Technical Report: Diagnosis and Management of Hyperbilirubinemia. Pediatrics 2022;150(3):e2022058865 (PMID 35927519; DOI 10.1542/peds.2022-058865); Puopolo KM et al. Management of Neonates Born at >=35 0/7 Weeks With Suspected/Proven Early-Onset Bacterial Sepsis. Pediatrics 2018;142(6):e20182894 (PMID 30455342); Adamkin DH. Postnatal Glucose Homeostasis in Late-Preterm and Term Infants. Pediatrics 2011;127(3):575-579 (PMID 21357346); Meek JY, Noble L. Breastfeeding and the Use of Human Milk. Pediatrics 2022;150(1) (PMID 35921640); AAP COFN. Hospital Stay for Healthy Term Newborn Infants. Pediatrics 2015;135(5):948-953.
evidence_grade: Strong for graded elements (Class I / Key Action Statements): AAP 2022 hyperbilirubinemia CPG (universal predischarge bilirubin, GA/risk-based phototherapy thresholds), IM vitamin K, hepatitis B birth dose, and universal newborn/CCHD/hearing screening are evidence-based standards of care. Several bundle elements (rooming-in, safe sleep, discharge timing, EOS-calculator use, dextrose gel) rest on AAP policy/consensus with observational/RCT support = Moderate-to-consensus. Overall: Strong (Class I society guideline core) with some consensus/expert components.
severity_cc_mcc_notes: DRG 795 is by definition the uncomplicated normal newborn (the no-problem tier). Complications shift the case OUT of 795 into the neonatal problem DRGs: 794 (neonate with other significant problems), 793 (full-term neonate with major problems), 792/791 (prematurity without/with MCC), 790 (extreme immaturity/RDS), 789 (neonate died or transferred <5 days). Practically, phototherapy-requiring hyperbilirubinemia, hypoglycemia needing IV dextrose, EOS/antibiotics, respiratory distress, or late-preterm status escalate LOS (often 3 to 7+ days), may trigger NICU/step-down, and re-group the case; therefore 795 optimization = keeping the newborn correctly in the uncomplicated tier by preventing or early-treating these problems.
pathway_pearls: (1) Universal predischarge bilirubin (TcB/TSB at 24-48h) plotted on the AAP 2022 GA/risk nomogram with a hardwired, risk-based follow-up time is the single biggest lever against the #1 readmission (jaundice); never discharge without it. (2) Use the neonatal EOS risk calculator to keep low-risk babies with mom and avoid unnecessary separation, labs, and antibiotics. (3) Protocolized 40% dextrose gel for asymptomatic at-risk hypoglycemia prevents avoidable NICU transfers and preserves breastfeeding. (4) Guarantee a 48-72h PCP follow-up (mandatory if discharged <48h); the early weight/jaundice/feeding recheck is what makes a safe short stay safe.

scope_and_volume_notes: Single largest inpatient category when neonates are included; ~3.0M of ~3.67M live births (2022 CDC). Remainder of births carry a problem/prematurity DRG (see rank 11). Approximate.
drg_code_titles_v43_1: 795 — Normal Newborn
volume_data_vintage: Primarily HCUP NIS 2021 national estimates; obstetric/newborn estimates cross-checked to CDC 2022 natality. Volumes were not re-estimated during the FY2026 code migration.
citation_audit_status: Completed 2026-07-21: 5/5 cited PMID references resolved in PubMed; CMS code existence and MDC/type metadata validated against v43.1; structured checks passed for risk thresholds, time targets, management detail, LOS, quality metrics, guideline dates, and citations.
clinical_verification_basis: Original independent adversarial high-stakes review completed; current pass revalidated citation resolvability and FY2026 coding/taxonomy.
data_quality_notes: Condition-level pathways may intentionally share DRGs; this is not a one-row-per-DRG crosswalk.

admission_criteria: Admit in active labor: regular painful contractions WITH cervical change and active phase >=6 cm dilation (Zhang/Consortium on Safe Labor curve; ACOG-SMFM Safe Prevention of Primary Cesarean); OR spontaneous rupture of membranes at term; OR indicated induction of labor (IOL) - gestational HTN/preeclampsia, pregestational/gestational diabetes, FGR, oligohydramnios, cholestasis, PROM, post-dates >=41 0/7 wk, or ARRIVE-type elective IOL >=39 0/7 wk in low-risk nullipara. Latent labor (<6 cm) without maternal/fetal indication -> offer therapeutic rest/outpatient management or delayed admission (lowers primary cesarean and oxytocin use). Non-reassuring fetal status, active vaginal bleeding, or maternal instability -> admit regardless of dilation.
risk_stratification: Bishop score <=6 = unfavorable cervix -> cervical ripening before oxytocin (>=8 favorable). NICHD/ACOG 3-tier FHR category (I reassuring / II indeterminate / III abnormal -> intrauterine resuscitation + expedited delivery). CMQCC/AWHONN hemorrhage risk tier (low/medium/high): medium -> type & screen; high (placenta previa/accreta spectrum, >=2 prior cesareans, active bleeding, coagulopathy, Hct <30, >=4 prior births) -> type & crossmatch + PPH-bundle readiness. Maternal Early Warning Criteria (Mhyre): SBP <90 or >160, DBP >100-110, HR <50 or >120, RR <10 or >30, SpO2 <95%, oliguria -> urgent bedside evaluation. ACOG severe-range BP >=160 systolic or >=110 diastolic sustained -> treat <=30-60 min. VTE: modified Caprini / ACOG-RCOG risk -> mechanical +/- LMWH. GBS status (screen 36 0/7-37 6/7 wk) drives intrapartum prophylaxis.
initial_workup_labs: CBC with platelets (baseline Hgb/Hct, thrombocytopenia); ABO/Rh type & antibody screen (Rh-negative -> plan anti-D/RhoGAM; positive screen -> antibody workup); confirm GBS vaginal-rectal culture result. Verify prenatal infectious labs: HIV, HBsAg, RPR/syphilis (repeat 3rd-trimester/at admission if high-risk), rubella & varicella immunity. If hypertensive -> preeclampsia labs: CBC/platelets, creatinine, AST/ALT, LDH, uric acid, +/- haptoglobin, urine protein:creatinine ratio (>=0.3) or 24h protein. High hemorrhage risk -> type & crossmatch + fibrinogen (<200 mg/dL predicts severe PPH). POC glucose if diabetic.
initial_imaging_dx: Electronic fetal monitoring - continuous EFM for higher-risk or intermittent auscultation for low-risk labor per ACOG (assess FHR + contraction pattern; interpret by NICHD 3-tier category). Point-of-care ultrasound to confirm fetal presentation/position when uncertain and to localize placenta. Digital cervical exam for dilation/effacement/station. Sterile speculum with nitrazine/ferning or fetal-fibronectin/AmniSure (PAMG-1) if rupture of membranes equivocal. No routine radiologic imaging.
time_critical_interventions: Active management of third stage - prophylactic oxytocin 10 IU IM or IV infusion within 1 min of delivery (~50% PPH reduction). GBS-positive -> IV penicillin G ideally >=4 h before delivery (do NOT delay indicated delivery for antibiotics; >=2 h reduces colony counts). Sustained severe-range HTN (>=160/110) -> IV labetalol/hydralazine or oral immediate-release nifedipine within 30-60 min. Category III tracing -> intrauterine resuscitation (reposition, O2, IV bolus, stop oxytocin, consider terbutaline) + expedited delivery. PPH -> quantitative blood loss (QBL) measurement, uterotonics, and tranexamic acid 1 g IV within 3 h of onset (WOMAN trial). Shoulder dystocia -> McRoberts + suprapubic pressure sequence. Delayed cord clamping >=30-60 s (ACOG CO 814). Skin-to-skin + breastfeeding within first hour.
initial_management: Analgesia: neuraxial (epidural/CSE) first-line - no minimum dilation to initiate; alternatives nitrous oxide, IV opioid (fentanyl/remifentanil PCA). Oxytocin for labor augmentation and active third-stage management. Uterotonic cascade for atony: oxytocin -> methylergonovine 0.2 mg IM (avoid if hypertensive) -> carboprost/Hemabate 250 mcg IM (avoid if asthma) -> misoprostol 600-1000 mcg; PLUS tranexamic acid 1 g IV. Isotonic crystalloid resuscitation; restrictive/physiology-driven transfusion (transfuse for hemodynamic instability or ongoing loss rather than a fixed Hgb; 1:1:1 RBC:FFP:platelets in massive transfusion). Source control escalation: uterine massage/bimanual compression, Bakri balloon or intrauterine vacuum-induced hemorrhage-control device (Jada; ACOG 2025 Clinical Practice Update), B-Lynch, uterine artery embolization, hysterectomy. Perineal laceration repair; single-dose 2nd-gen cephalosporin for OASIS (3rd/4th degree). GBS prophylaxis: penicillin G (cefazolin if low-risk allergy; clindamycin if susceptible/vancomycin if not, for anaphylaxis risk). Rh-negative -> anti-D immune globulin.
day1_milestones: LABOR/DELIVERY (Day 0): admit, confirm dating/presentation, establish FHR category and IV access, draw admission labs, provide neuraxial analgesia on request; oxytocin augmentation guided by Zhang labor curve; GBS prophylaxis if indicated; support spontaneous vs directed pushing in 2nd stage; delivery with active third-stage management + QBL; immediate skin-to-skin, delayed cord clamping >=30-60 s, initiate breastfeeding within 1 h; perineal repair; activate PPH huddle/bundle if QBL >=1000 mL or hemodynamic change; scheduled fundal/lochia checks and vitals per postpartum protocol.
day2_milestones: POSTPARTUM DAY 1: reassess uterine tone/fundal height, lochia, perineum/repair; ensure spontaneous voiding and remove Foley (watch for urinary retention post-neuraxial); ambulate once motor block resolves; advance to regular diet; transition to opioid-sparing oral analgesia (scheduled acetaminophen + NSAID); recheck CBC if hemorrhage/anemia; continue BP surveillance (especially hypertensive disorders) and MgSO4 if on seizure prophylaxis; continue mechanical VTE prophylaxis +/- LMWH; lactation support; confirm anti-D given if Rh-negative; begin discharge and newborn-care teaching + POST-BIRTH warning signs.
day3plus_milestones: Most uncomplicated vaginal deliveries discharge on postpartum day 1-2 (>=24 h). Confirm all discharge criteria: afebrile, hemodynamically stable, BP controlled, ambulating, voiding, tolerating diet, pain controlled on oral meds, firm fundus/normal lochia, competent in self and newborn care. Administer indicated immunizations (Tdap if not given antepartum, MMR if rubella-nonimmune, varicella if nonimmune); initiate/plan contraception; complete depression screen (EPDS/PHQ-9); give anti-D if not yet administered. Prolonged stay to day 3+ reserved for complications - severe preeclampsia/eclampsia, PPH requiring transfusion, endometritis, OASIS wound issues, or significant maternal comorbidity.
consults_multidisciplinary: Anesthesiology for neuraxial analgesia and hemorrhage/airway management; Pediatrics/Neonatology present at delivery for at-risk births (meconium, non-reassuring FHR, preterm, shoulder dystocia, operative vaginal delivery); Lactation/IBCLC; Maternal-Fetal Medicine for high-risk comorbidity (severe preeclampsia, placenta accreta spectrum, cardiac disease); Social work for psychosocial risk, substance use, newborn safety; Pharmacy for anticoagulation and uterotonic dosing; Nutrition if diabetic; Case management/care coordination for postpartum follow-up and transitions of care; Behavioral health/Psychiatry if positive depression screen or thoughts of self-harm.
monitoring_level: Routine labor on L&D with continuous or intermittent FHR plus maternal vitals; postpartum on mother-baby (couplet) unit with Maternal Early Warning Criteria-driven escalation. Escalate to obstetric ICU/step-down for: massive hemorrhage or ongoing transfusion, DIC, eclampsia or severe preeclampsia with end-organ dysfunction (pulmonary edema, AKI, HELLP), sepsis, cardiac decompensation, or need for vasopressors/invasive monitoring. Rapid-response and OB hemorrhage/severe-hypertension bundle activation on any MEWC trigger.
nutrition_mobility_vte: Diet: oral intake as tolerated in labor (clear liquids reasonable for low-risk per ACOG/ASA), regular diet immediately postpartum (or after repair of complex laceration). Mobility: early ambulation once neuraxial motor block resolves. VTE: mechanical prophylaxis (sequential compression devices) for all during labor/immobility; pharmacologic LMWH reserved for high-risk (prior VTE, high-risk thrombophilia, elevated Caprini, or cesarean conversion) - uncomplicated vaginal delivery is low baseline risk. Glucose control if diabetic (target <180 mg/dL). Early removal of IV and Foley; monitor for post-neuraxial urinary retention. Bowel regimen/stool softeners, especially after OASIS.
discharge_criteria: Afebrile and hemodynamically stable; BP controlled (no sustained severe-range readings; <150/100 for hypertensive disorders); uterus firm/contracted with normal-volume lochia; adequate spontaneous voiding without retention; pain controlled on oral analgesics; tolerating regular diet and ambulating independently; perineum/repair intact without expanding hematoma or infection; Hgb acceptable or anemia addressed; able to void, ambulate, and care for self and newborn; newborn cleared for discharge; adequate home support and follow-up arranged. Minimum ~24 h observation; federal Newborns' and Mothers' Health Protection Act guarantees >=48 h of coverage.
discharge_planning: Disposition home with newborn. Medication reconciliation: opioid-sparing oral analgesia (acetaminophen/NSAID, limited opioids), iron if anemic, prenatal vitamins, antihypertensives if hypertensive disorder, stool softener. Immunizations: Tdap, MMR (rubella-nonimmune), varicella (nonimmune); anti-D if Rh-negative and infant Rh-positive. Contraception counseling/initiation (immediate postpartum LARC, progestin-only, or combined per breastfeeding/VTE timing). Follow-up scheduling: BP check within 72 h and again at 7-10 days for hypertensive disorders; postpartum contact within first 3 wks and comprehensive visit by 12 wks (ACOG fourth-trimester model); newborn visit at 48-72 h. Education: AWHONN POST-BIRTH warning signs, lochia/fever/incision care, feeding support, safe infant sleep, and depression screening (EPDS) with referral pathway; lactation resources and transitions-of-care handoff to primary medical home.
expected_los: ~2 days for uncomplicated vaginal delivery (MS-DRG 807 [no CC/MCC] GMLOS ~2.0 days; DRG 806 [CC] ~2.3-2.5 days; DRG 805 [MCC] ~3-4 days; delivery-with-sterilization/D&C DRGs 796/797/798 run marginally longer). Federal Newborns' and Mothers' Health Protection Act guarantees >=48 h of insurance coverage after vaginal birth.
target_los: 1.5-2 days (~24-48 h). Safe early discharge at 24-48 h for stable, uncomplicated mothers meeting all discharge criteria with newborn readiness and reliable follow-up; do not discharge before 24 h unless every criterion is met and close follow-up assured.
quality_metrics: Joint Commission/CMS Perinatal Care: PC-01 (elective delivery <39 wk), PC-02 (NTSV/low-risk primary cesarean; eCQM), PC-05 (exclusive breast-milk feeding), PC-06 (unexpected complications in term newborns), PC-07 (severe obstetric complications). AHRQ PSI-18/PSI-19 (obstetric trauma - 3rd/4th degree laceration with/without instrument). Severe maternal morbidity index and transfusion rate (CMQCC/CDC); timely treatment of severe-range hypertension (<=60 min); PPH recognition via QBL; CMS Birthing-Friendly Hospital designation and Maternal Morbidity Structural Measure; 30-day postpartum readmission; postpartum depression screening rate.
common_complications: Postpartum hemorrhage/uterine atony (mitigate: admission risk stratification, active third-stage management, QBL, hemorrhage cart/bundle, TXA <=3 h); perineal lacerations/OASIS (mitigate: restrictive episiotomy, warm perineal compress, prophylactic antibiotic for OASIS, meticulous repair); endometritis/wound infection (mitigate: aseptic technique, prophylaxis for OASIS/manual placenta removal, early recognition); preeclampsia/eclampsia (mitigate: BP surveillance, magnesium sulfate seizure prophylaxis, timely severe-HTN treatment); urinary retention (post-neuraxial voiding trial before Foley removal); VTE (mechanical +/- LMWH); anemia; postpartum depression (EPDS screening).
readmission_drivers: Hypertensive disorders/postpartum preeclampsia - leading cause of postpartum readmission (levers: discharge BP <150/100, antihypertensive at discharge for HDP, BP recheck at 72 h and 7-10 days, warning-sign education); infection - endometritis, perineal/wound, mastitis (levers: prophylaxis, hygiene and lactation education); secondary/delayed PPH and symptomatic anemia (levers: iron repletion, QBL documentation, early follow-up); VTE (levers: risk-based LMWH, symptom education); uncontrolled pain and wound breakdown; mood disorders (levers: EPDS screening + referral). Structured transitions, early postpartum contact (<=3 wk), and AWHONN POST-BIRTH warning-sign teaching reduce bounce-backs.
guideline_source: ACOG (American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists), with SMFM, AWHONN/CMQCC maternal safety bundles, and Joint Commission Perinatal Care measures: ACOG Practice Bulletin 183 Postpartum Hemorrhage (2017); ACOG/SMFM Obstetric Care Consensus No. 1 Safe Prevention of the Primary Cesarean Delivery (2014, reaffirmed 2019); ACOG Committee Opinion 797 Prevention of Group B Streptococcal Early-Onset Disease (2020); Committee Opinion 767 Emergent Therapy for Acute-Onset Severe Hypertension (2019); Committee Opinion 736 Optimizing Postpartum Care (2018); Committee Opinion 814 Delayed Umbilical Cord Clamping (2020); Practice Bulletin 196 Thromboembolism in Pregnancy (2018); ACOG Clinical Practice Update on Nonsurgical Hemorrhage-Control Devices (2025).
key_citations: ACOG Practice Bulletin No. 183 Postpartum Hemorrhage, Obstet Gynecol 2017;130:e168-e186 (PMID 28937571); WOMAN Trial: Effect of early tranexamic acid on mortality/hysterectomy in PPH, Lancet 2017;389:2105-2116 - TXA within 3 h reduces death from bleeding (PMID 28456509; DOI 10.1016/S0140-6736(17)30638-4); ACOG/SMFM Safe Prevention of the Primary Cesarean Delivery, Obstet Gynecol 2014;123:693-711 (PMC4210674); ARRIVE Trial (Grobman WA et al.), Labor Induction vs Expectant Management in Low-Risk Nulliparous Women, N Engl J Med 2018;379:513-523 (PMID 30089070); ACOG Committee Opinion 797 GBS Prevention 2020 (PMID 31977795; DOI 10.1097/AOG.0000000000003668); ACOG Committee Opinion 736 Optimizing Postpartum Care 2018 (PMID 29683911; DOI 10.1097/AOG.0000000000002633); ACOG Committee Opinion 767 Emergent Therapy for Acute-Onset Severe Hypertension 2019 (PMID 30575639); ACOG Committee Opinion 814 Delayed Umbilical Cord Clamping 2020 (PMID 33214530); ACOG Practice Bulletin 196 Thromboembolism in Pregnancy 2018 (PMID 29939938). Per PubMed.
evidence_grade: Strong - anchored on multiple current ACOG Practice Bulletins/Committee Opinions and ACOG/SMFM Obstetric Care Consensus (Level A-B recommendations) reinforced by Class I RCT evidence (WOMAN trial for TXA within 3 h; ARRIVE for 39-week elective induction in low-risk nulliparas). Select operational elements are quality-collaborative/consensus grade (Level B-C): Maternal Early Warning Criteria thresholds, CMQCC/AWHONN 3-tier hemorrhage risk stratification, and quantitative-blood-loss practice - moderate evidence but broadly endorsed safety-bundle standards.
severity_cc_mcc_notes: Severity tiering drives LOS and intensity across the collapsed group. No-CC/MCC (DRG 807, and 775/798): uncomplicated pathway, LOS ~2 days, mother-baby unit, no ICU. CC tier (DRG 806, 774, 797): e.g., 2nd/3rd-degree lacerations, mild-moderate anemia, controlled HDP, mild infection -> LOS ~2.3-2.5 days, closer monitoring. MCC tier (DRG 805, 796): eclampsia/severe preeclampsia with end-organ dysfunction, severe PPH requiring transfusion/DIC, sepsis, pulmonary edema, or peripartum hysterectomy -> LOS ~3-4+ days with possible obstetric ICU/step-down and MFM/anesthesia/critical-care involvement. Delivery-with-sterilization/D&C DRGs (796/797/798) add a tubal procedure; DRG 768 captures vaginal delivery with an unrelated OR procedure - both slightly extend stay and analgesia needs. DRG 774/775 (with/without complicating diagnoses) coexist with the CC/MCC-tiered set in the post-FY2020 grouper.
pathway_pearls: 1) Do not admit latent labor (<6 cm) without indication - use the 6 cm active-phase threshold and Zhang labor curve to cut the primary cesarean rate (the single biggest downstream cost/morbidity lever). 2) Hemorrhage readiness is a system, not a reaction: admission risk stratification + quantitative blood loss + hemorrhage bundle/cart + tranexamic acid within 3 h; oxytocin is always first-line and TXA is time-critical. 3) Treat sustained severe-range hypertension (>=160/110) within 30-60 min every time - postpartum preeclampsia is the leading readmission driver, so pair inpatient control with a 72-h and 7-10-day BP follow-up plan. 4) Extend care past the delivery: fourth-trimester follow-up (contact <=3 wk, comprehensive by 12 wk), POST-BIRTH warning-sign education, RhoGAM/vaccines/contraception before discharge, and EPDS depression screening.

admission_criteria: Sepsis-3: suspected/documented infection PLUS acute SOFA rise >=2 (organ dysfunction) -> inpatient admit; septic shock (vasopressor-dependent to keep MAP>=65 + lactate>2 mmol/L despite >=30 mL/kg fluids) -> ICU. Admit for lactate>=2 mmol/L, hypotension (SBP<90/MAP<65), new hypoxia/AKI/altered mentation, bandemia, or need for IV antibiotics with monitoring. Observation/ED discharge only for uncomplicated infection WITHOUT organ dysfunction, well-appearing, lactate<2, NEWS<5, tolerating PO with reliable follow-up (e.g., simple pyelonephritis/cellulitis). Lower threshold with immunocompromise, age>=65, indwelling devices, or poor social support.
risk_stratification: SSC 2026 (strong): screen high-risk acutely ill patients with NEWS/NEWS2/MEWS/SIRS, NOT qSOFA alone (low sensitivity). qSOFA>=2 of 3 (RR>=22, SBP<=100, GCS<15) flags high risk. SOFA acute rise>=2 defines sepsis-related organ dysfunction. Lactate 2-4 mmol/L = hypoperfusion (resuscitate + serial lactate); >=4 mmol/L = overt/cryptic shock, high mortality -> ICU. Septic shock = vasopressor-requiring MAP<65 + lactate>2 despite fluids (~30-40% mortality). APACHE II / SOFA for ICU prognostication; NEWS>=7 or acute rise -> rapid response / ICU evaluation.
initial_workup_labs: STAT serum lactate (repeat q2-4h if >=2 mmol/L until clearance); blood cultures x2 from separate sites BEFORE antibiotics (SEP-1); CBC with differential (WBC/bands, platelets for SOFA/DIC); CMP (creatinine/BUN for AKI, bilirubin/LFTs, anion-gap/bicarbonate); coagulation panel PT-INR/aPTT/fibrinogen/D-dimer (DIC screen); VBG/ABG; source cultures (urinalysis+urine culture, sputum/respiratory viral panel, wound, CSF if meningitis suspected); baseline procalcitonin (trend for de-escalation, NOT to withhold empiric antibiotics); CRP; troponin/BNP if cardiac dysfunction suspected.
initial_imaging_dx: Portable CXR STAT (pneumonia/ARDS). Source-directed imaging emergently once stabilized: CT abdomen/pelvis with contrast for intra-abdominal/urinary source; RUQ ultrasound / MRCP for biliary source; CT chest; soft-tissue CT/MRI for necrotizing infection or abscess. Point-of-care ultrasound (POCUS) at bedside for dynamic fluid-responsiveness (IVC, VTI), cardiac function, and lung. TTE if septic cardiomyopathy or persistent shock. LP if CNS source suspected (do not delay antibiotics for imaging/LP).
time_critical_interventions: Hour-1 / SEP-1 bundle: (1) measure lactate, repeat at 2-6h if >2 mmol/L targeting clearance/normalization; (2) blood cultures BEFORE antibiotics; (3) broad-spectrum IV antibiotics <=1h for septic shock AND high-likelihood sepsis (SSC 2026 strong), <=3h for possible sepsis without shock (conditional); (4) >=30 mL/kg balanced crystalloid within 3h for hypotension or lactate>=4; (5) vasopressors within 1h of shock recognition to restore MAP>=65 (may start peripherally). Emergent SOURCE CONTROL as soon as feasible, ideally <6-12h (drain abscess, remove infected line, relieve obstruction).
initial_management: Fluids: balanced crystalloids (lactated Ringer's/Plasma-Lyte) over 0.9% saline (SSC 2026 upgraded; SMART) — >=30 mL/kg then titrate to DYNAMIC measures (passive-leg-raise, SVV, POCUS), avoid indiscriminate boluses (CLOVERS: early norepinephrine acceptable). Empiric antibiotics by source/risk: anti-pseudomonal beta-lactam (piperacillin-tazobactam, cefepime, or meropenem) as EXTENDED/prolonged infusion after loading dose (2026 strong) + MRSA coverage (vancomycin or linezolid) when risk factors; echinocandin if invasive-candidiasis risk; de-escalate at 48-72h on cultures + procalcitonin. Vasopressors: norepinephrine FIRST-LINE to MAP>=65; add vasopressin 0.03 U/min at norepi ~0.25-0.5 mcg/kg/min; then epinephrine; angiotensin II for refractory shock. Dobutamine/epinephrine if sepsis-induced cardiomyopathy with hypoperfusion. IV hydrocortisone 200 mg/day (50 mg q6h or continuous) for vasopressor-dependent shock (ADRENAL/APROCCHSS). Restrictive transfusion Hgb<7 g/dL. Glucose target <=180 mg/dL. Definitive source control.
day1_milestones: Complete SEP-1 bundle; confirm source and achieve/plan source control; restore MAP>=65, lactate clearance (>=10-20% per 2h toward normal), UOP>0.5 mL/kg/h, improving mentation. Begin vasopressor weaning as perfusion restored; transition from resuscitation to de-resuscitation (avoid positive fluid balance). Reassess empiric antibiotics against preliminary micro; obtain source-control input. Start VTE prophylaxis, stress-ulcer prophylaxis if indicated, glucose control (<=180). Set ICU vs step-down disposition.
day2_milestones: Off or actively weaning vasopressors with sustained MAP>=65; lactate normalized. Narrow/de-escalate antibiotics per culture + susceptibilities (SSC 2026 upgraded to recommendation); taper hydrocortisone as shock resolves. Active de-resuscitation/diuresis if fluid-overloaded. Reassess organ dysfunction (renal — RRT need?, respiratory — extubation readiness). Start enteral nutrition and early mobilization/PT; daily sedation interruption (SAT) + spontaneous breathing trial (SBT) if ventilated. Remove unnecessary central lines/urinary catheters.
day3plus_milestones: Vasopressor-free, defervescing, stable on floor. Transition IV->PO antibiotics when hemodynamically stable + tolerating PO + source controlled; fix TOTAL duration (procalcitonin-guided; ~7 days for most bacteremia/pneumonia/intra-abdominal with source control, longer for endocarditis, undrained focus, or S. aureus bacteremia). Step down ICU->floor. Resolve/assess residual organ dysfunction (AKI recovery vs RRT, ventilator liberation). Screen for post-sepsis syndrome (delirium, ICU-acquired weakness, functional decline); engage PT/OT, nutrition, and discharge planning.
consults_multidisciplinary: Critical care/intensivist for shock or organ failure. Infectious Diseases for bacteremia, resistant/fungal organisms, endocarditis, immunocompromise, or unclear source. Source-control proceduralists early: general surgery (intra-abdominal, necrotizing soft-tissue), interventional radiology (abscess drainage), urology (obstructive uropathy/pyonephrosis), GI/ERCP (ascending cholangitis). Antimicrobial-stewardship pharmacist (empiric selection, PK/PD and extended-infusion dosing, de-escalation). PT/OT for early mobility; nutrition for enteral feeding; care management + social work for post-acute disposition; palliative care for goals-of-care in refractory shock/multi-organ failure.
monitoring_level: ICU for septic shock (any vasopressor), lactate>=4 mmol/L, mechanical ventilation, or >=2 organ dysfunctions. Step-down/intermediate for resolving shock weaning a single low-dose vasopressor or requiring high-flow oxygen. Telemetry/floor for sepsis WITHOUT shock, stable hemodynamics, single-organ dysfunction responding to therapy. Escalate (rapid response/ICU) for: recurrent MAP<65 or new vasopressor need, rising/non-clearing lactate, NEWS>=7 or acute rise, new hypoxemic/respiratory failure, oliguria/AKI progression, or new altered mentation.
nutrition_mobility_vte: Nutrition: early enteral feeding within 24-48h (trophic/low-dose acceptable during shock; avoid early parenteral). Mobility: early mobilization + PT once hemodynamically stable; daily sedation minimization with SAT/SBT if ventilated to prevent ICU-acquired weakness and delirium. VTE prophylaxis: pharmacologic LMWH (e.g., enoxaparin) preferred over UFH unless active bleeding/severe thrombocytopenia -> mechanical (SCDs). Glucose: insulin protocol targeting <=180 mg/dL (avoid <110 / hypoglycemia). Lines/catheters: remove central venous and urinary catheters as soon as no longer required (daily necessity review) to reduce CLABSI/CAUTI.
discharge_criteria: Hemodynamically stable off vasopressors >=24h; afebrile or clearly defervescing with source controlled; transitioned to a stable oral regimen (or arranged OPAT/IV plan) and tolerating oral intake/medications; lactate normalized and organ dysfunction resolving (stable/improving creatinine, adequate oxygenation off high-flow); mentation at baseline with safe mobility/functional status (PT clearance); reliable follow-up secured and patient understands return precautions.
discharge_planning: Disposition matched to function: home (with services) vs SNF/acute rehab/LTACH for deconditioning or ongoing needs. Medication reconciliation with EXPLICIT antibiotic stop date; arrange OPAT (ID follow-up + weekly safety labs) if completing an IV course. Schedule PCP follow-up within 7 days plus ID/specialty and repeat labs as needed. Educate on return precautions (recurrent-infection/sepsis red flags), post-sepsis syndrome (fatigue, weakness, cognitive/mood changes), and device care. Provide transition-of-care summary with warm handoff and a care-management follow-up call to reduce 30-day readmission.
expected_los: CMS GMLOS by DRG: 872 (no CC/MCC) ~3.6 days; 871 (with MCC) ~5.1 days; 870 (severe sepsis/septic shock with MV>96h) ~13.5 days. Blended non-ventilated sepsis ~4-6 days; AMLOS runs higher (~6-8 days) due to complicated outliers.
target_los: Optimized under a bundle-compliant pathway: DRG 872 ~3 days; DRG 871 ~4-4.5 days; DRG 870 (prolonged MV) ~11-12 days. Levers: early source control, rapid antibiotic de-escalation, timely IV->PO switch, and active de-resuscitation to shorten ICU/ventilator days.
quality_metrics: CMS SEP-1 (Severe Sepsis/Septic Shock Early Management Bundle) — all-or-none composite, now transitioned into the Hospital Value-Based Purchasing Program (FY2026 payment). Process: time-to-antibiotics, blood cultures before antibiotics, 30 mL/kg fluid for hypotension/lactate>=4, repeat lactate, vasopressor timing. Outcome: risk-adjusted sepsis mortality (O/E), 30-day mortality, 30-day readmission. Safety/HAC: CLABSI, CAUTI, C. difficile; appropriate empiric therapy + de-escalation (stewardship). Operational: ICU LOS and ventilator days.
common_complications: Septic-shock progression / multi-organ dysfunction (MODS); acute kidney injury (+/- RRT); ARDS / respiratory failure; DIC with thrombocytopenia; sepsis-induced cardiomyopathy; delirium and ICU-acquired weakness; secondary nosocomial infection (VAP, CLABSI, CAUTI, C. difficile); iatrogenic fluid overload. Mitigation: bundle-driven early antibiotics + source control, balanced-crystalloid and dynamic-guided (not indiscriminate) fluids with de-resuscitation, lung-protective ventilation, daily line/catheter review, SAT/SBT + early mobility, and antimicrobial stewardship.
readmission_drivers: Top drivers: recurrent/relapsed or inadequately source-controlled infection; C. difficile and antibiotic-associated complications; post-sepsis syndrome (functional decline, delirium, new-infection readmission); comorbidity decompensation (HF, CKD, diabetes); dehydration/AKI; premature discharge before hemodynamic or PO-antibiotic stability. Prevention levers: definitive source control, right-duration/right-route antibiotics with OPAT + ID follow-up, medication reconciliation, PCP visit within 7 days, post-sepsis education, and care-management transition calls.
guideline_source: PRIMARY: Surviving Sepsis Campaign (SCCM/ESICM) International Guidelines for Management of Sepsis and Septic Shock 2026 (current; supersedes SSC 2021). Regulatory: CMS SEP-1 Severe Sepsis and Septic Shock Early Management Bundle (now in Hospital Value-Based Purchasing, FY2026). Definitions: Sepsis-3 Consensus (JAMA 2016).
key_citations: Surviving Sepsis Campaign 2026: Crit Care Med 2026;54:725-812 (PMID 41869847, DOI 10.1097/CCM.0000000000007075) and Intensive Care Med 2026;52:863-936 (PMID 41870560, DOI 10.1007/s00134-026-08361-1); SSC 2021: Intensive Care Med 2021;47:1181-1247 (PMID 34599691); Sepsis-3 Singer JAMA 2016;315:801-810 (PMID 26903338); Kumar antibiotic-timing Crit Care Med 2006;34:1589-1596 (PMID 16625125); SMART balanced crystalloids Semler NEJM 2018;378:829-839 (PMID 29485925); CLOVERS restrictive-vs-liberal fluids NEJM 2023;388:499-510 (PMID 36688507); ADRENAL hydrocortisone Venkatesh NEJM 2018;378:797-808 (PMID 29347874); SEPSISPAM MAP target Asfar NEJM 2014;370:1583-1593 (PMID 24635770). Citations verified via PubMed.
evidence_grade: Strong overall — anchored to a current Class I international society guideline (SSC 2026, GRADE methodology) plus a CMS regulatory bundle. Important caveat: several high-profile bundle elements are STRONG recommendations resting on LOW/very-low-certainty evidence (1-hour antibiotics, >=30 mL/kg fluids), and several are conditional (corticosteroids, vasopressin timing, peripheral vasopressors); balanced crystalloids and MAP 65 rest on moderate certainty.
severity_cc_mcc_notes: Collapsed group spans a wide severity range. DRG 872 (no CC/MCC): sepsis without major organ failure -> telemetry/floor, GMLOS ~3.6 days, low mortality. DRG 871 (with MCC — e.g., acute respiratory/renal failure, septic shock, encephalopathy): severe sepsis -> ICU/step-down, GMLOS ~5.1 days, higher mortality/cost. DRG 870 (severe sepsis/septic shock requiring MV>96h): sickest tier -> prolonged ICU, GMLOS ~13.5 days, highest mortality (~30-40%) and resource use. Accurate documentation of acute organ dysfunctions/shock drives correct MCC capture, which reflects true severity and determines ICU need, LOS, and reimbursement.
pathway_pearls: 1) SPEED beats everything: cultures then broad-spectrum antibiotics within 1h of shock recognition alongside balanced-crystalloid resuscitation — each hour of antibiotic delay in shock raises mortality (Kumar 2006). 2) SOURCE CONTROL is decisive — no antibiotic salvages an undrained abscess, obstructed system, or infected device; engage proceduralists within 6-12h. 3) Resuscitate then DE-RESUSCITATE — dynamic-guided balanced crystalloids with early peripheral norepinephrine to MAP>=65 (SSC 2026), avoiding the fluid overload that drives ARDS/AKI. 4) Right-drug/right-duration STEWARDSHIP — de-escalate on cultures, procalcitonin-guided ~7-day courses, early IV->PO to shorten LOS and cut C. difficile.

admission_criteria: Admit (severe COVID) if SpO2 <94% on room air at sea level, new/escalating supplemental O2 need, RR >30/min, PaO2/FiO2 <300, or >3-4% desaturation with ambulation; also admit for hemodynamic instability, altered mentation, end-organ hypoperfusion, inability to maintain oral intake, or decompensating high-risk comorbidity; ED discharge/observation acceptable if SpO2 >=94% RA with stable vitals, reliable follow-up and ability to self-isolate (observe borderline SpO2 92-94% or exertional desaturation); direct ICU/step-down if requiring HFNC/NIV, rapidly rising FiO2, or shock
risk_stratification: ISARIC 4C Mortality Score (age, sex, comorbidities, RR, SpO2, GCS, urea, CRP): 0-3 Low (1.2% mortality) floor care; 4-8 Intermediate (9.9%); 9-14 High (31.4%) close monitoring/step-down; >=15 Very high (61.5%) ICU-level + goals-of-care; NIH clinical severity tier (moderate SpO2 >=94%; severe SpO2 <94% or RR>30 or P/F<300 or infiltrates >50%; critical = respiratory failure/shock/MODS) drives drug tier; NEWS2 >=5 escalate and >=7 urgent critical-care review; 4C Deterioration Score for in-hospital decline; qSOFA >=2 / SOFA for sepsis; adverse-prognosis labs = lymphopenia, D-dimer/CRP/ferritin/LDH/troponin elevation
initial_workup_labs: SARS-CoV-2 NAAT/PCR (or antigen) confirmation; CBC with differential (lymphopenia = worse prognosis; neutrophilia suggests bacterial co-infection); CMP with baseline eGFR and LFTs (before remdesivir); inflammatory panel CRP (immunomodulator eligibility, e.g. >=75 mg/L), ferritin, LDH, D-dimer (elevation triggers PE workup + anticoagulation intensity decision); procalcitonin (withhold antibiotics if low); coagulation PT/INR/aPTT/fibrinogen; troponin and BNP/NT-proBNP (myocardial injury); ABG/VBG in severe disease for P/F ratio; blood cultures + respiratory viral panel (influenza/RSV) if co-infection suspected; glucose/HbA1c (steroid hyperglycemia risk)
initial_imaging_dx: Portable chest X-ray on admission (bilateral peripheral ground-glass/infiltrates); CT pulmonary angiography if elevated/rising D-dimer plus clinical suspicion of PE (COVID hypercoagulability); CT chest otherwise reserved for diagnostic uncertainty/complications; point-of-care lung ultrasound (bilateral B-lines, bedside monitoring without transport); 12-lead ECG on admission (baseline QTc, screen myocarditis/arrhythmia); TTE if elevated troponin, hemodynamic instability or suspected myocarditis/RV strain; continuous pulse oximetry plus ambulatory (walking) SpO2 to detect exertional desaturation
time_critical_interventions: Titrate O2 to target SpO2 92-96% promptly (avoid hyperoxia >96%); start dexamethasone same day once on supplemental O2 (RECOVERY mortality benefit); initiate remdesivir early, ideally <=7-10 days from symptom onset (earlier = greater benefit); add immunomodulator (tocilizumab or baricitinib) early for rapidly escalating O2 or systemic inflammation (CRP >=75) within ~24-96h; stepwise escalation NC->HFNC->awake prone positioning->NIV->intubation without delay (awake proning on HFNC reduces intubation, Ehrmann meta-trial); if concurrent bacterial septic shock, antibiotics <=1h with SEP-1 bundle (lactate + cultures first); VTE prophylaxis ordered within 24h
initial_management: O2 titrated to SpO2 92-96%, escalate NC->HFNC->NIV->invasive; corticosteroid dexamethasone 6 mg IV/PO daily up to 10 days ONLY if on supplemental O2/ventilation (prednisone 40/methylprednisolone 32/hydrocortisone 160 mg if unavailable); antiviral remdesivir 200 mg IV load then 100 mg IV daily x5 days (avoid monotherapy once HFNC+); immunomodulator added to dexamethasone for rapidly progressing/high-O2/CRP>=75 = tocilizumab 8 mg/kg IV (max 800 mg) x1 OR baricitinib 4 mg PO daily up to 14 days (renally dosed); alternatives tofacitinib, sarilumab; anticoagulation = therapeutic-dose LMWH/UFH for non-critically-ill with elevated D-dimer and low bleeding risk, prophylactic-dose for critically-ill (therapeutic harmful in ICU); antibiotics ONLY for documented/strong bacterial co-infection (procalcitonin-guided); conservative fluids; insulin for steroid hyperglycemia
day1_milestones: Confirm diagnosis, assign NIH severity tier and 4C score, establish code status/goals of care; select O2 modality to target SpO2 92-96% and place on continuous pulse oximetry; start dexamethasone if hypoxemic plus remdesivir; assess immunomodulator eligibility (O2 trajectory, CRP); order VTE prophylaxis/anticoagulation per severity; baseline labs (CRP/ferritin/D-dimer/LDH/troponin), CXR and ECG; awake proning if severe hypoxemia; ID/critical-care consult if escalating; screen for bacterial co-infection (procalcitonin/cultures) before any antibiotics
day2_milestones: Reassess O2 trajectory and work of breathing; trend inflammatory markers (CRP, D-dimer, ferritin); add second immunomodulator (tocilizumab or baricitinib) if rapidly escalating O2 or rising CRP and not already given; continue remdesivir and dexamethasone; monitor glucose with insulin sliding scale; re-evaluate for deterioration and escalate to HFNC/NIV/ICU per NEWS2 and P/F triggers; begin early mobilization/PT if stable; continue VTE prophylaxis; reassess anticoagulation intensity if D-dimer rising or PE suspected
day3plus_milestones: Wean supplemental O2 toward room-air SpO2 >=94% (or stable home-O2 level); complete remdesivir 5-day course; continue dexamethasone through day 10 (stop at discharge if O2-free); de-escalate/stop empiric antibiotics if cultures negative and procalcitonin low; progressive mobilization with PT/OT and assess home-O2 need and post-COVID sequelae; confirm afebrile >=24-48h and stable/declining O2 for >=24h for discharge readiness; execute discharge planning (home O2, follow-up, education, isolation)
consults_multidisciplinary: Infectious Diseases (antiviral/immunomodulator selection, antimicrobial stewardship); Pulmonary/Critical Care (escalation, HFNC/NIV/intubation, proning, ICU triage); Nephrology if AKI/RRT, Cardiology if myocardial injury/arrhythmia, Hematology for complex anticoagulation/HIT; Pharmacy (renal dosing, drug interactions, steroid/insulin, anticoagulation); PT/OT (early mobilization, deconditioning, functional assessment); dietitian/Nutrition (enteral nutrition if intubated, catabolic state); care management/social work (home O2, isolation logistics, disposition); Palliative care for very-high-risk/critical patients and goals-of-care
monitoring_level: Floor/med-surg with continuous pulse oximetry if stable on low-flow NC (<=6 L/min) and SpO2 >=92%; step-down/telemetry for moderate-high O2 needs, HFNC in capable units, escalating trajectory, or high 4C/NEWS2; ICU for high-setting HFNC, NIV, invasive ventilation, ECMO, vasopressors/shock, MODS, or P/F <150; escalation triggers = SpO2 <92% despite escalating O2, RR >30, rising FiO2, NEWS2 >=7, worsening P/F, hemodynamic instability or altered mentation prompting rapid response/ICU transfer
nutrition_mobility_vte: VTE = pharmacologic prophylaxis for all inpatients unless contraindicated (enoxaparin 40 mg SC daily), therapeutic-dose heparin for select non-critically-ill with elevated D-dimer and low bleeding risk, prophylactic-dose in ICU/critically-ill; mobility = early out-of-bed mobilization as tolerated, awake prone positioning for hypoxemia, PT for deconditioning; nutrition = diet as tolerated, early enteral nutrition if intubated, monitor catabolism; glucose target 140-180 mg/dL with insulin for dexamethasone-induced hyperglycemia; remove central lines and Foley catheters as early as possible to reduce CLABSI/CAUTI
discharge_criteria: SpO2 >=94% on room air (or stable at home-O2 baseline) with stable/declining O2 requirement for >=24h; afebrile and clinically improving with normalizing RR and stable vitals; able to maintain oral intake/hydration and ambulate at baseline (or with home O2); no need for higher level of care or continuous monitoring and comorbidities stable; adequate home support/isolation capacity and follow-up arranged; remdesivir course completed inpatient or transition arranged
discharge_planning: Disposition home (with home O2 via oximetry-triggered DME setup if needed) or SNF/rehab if deconditioned; provide pulse oximeter with clear return precautions (worsening dyspnea, SpO2 drop); medication reconciliation completing/appropriately stopping dexamethasone with no routine post-discharge steroids or antibiotics and post-discharge VTE prophylaxis only for select high-risk (extended prophylaxis not routine); follow-up PCP/telehealth within 7 days plus pulmonary and post-COVID/long-COVID clinic for persistent symptoms; patient education on isolation, symptom monitoring, glucose if on steroids, and when to seek care; vaccination counseling and transitions-of-care handoff
expected_los: Approx DRG GMLOS: 177 (w MCC) ~6.0 days, 178 (w CC) ~4.9 days, 179 (wo CC/MCC) ~3.7 days; real-world 2021 Delta/Omicron severe/hypoxemic COVID mean ~6-8 days and median ~5-6 days; ICU/mechanically ventilated cases 10-14+ days; typical oxygen-requiring inpatient stay ~5-7 days
target_los: ~4-5 days for moderate-to-severe oxygen-requiring COVID under the ideal pathway (early antiviral + steroid + timely immunomodulator, oximetry-guided O2 weaning, oximetry-triggered home-O2 discharge program); critical/ventilated tier remains longer, governed by ventilation and organ recovery
quality_metrics: 30-day all-cause readmission; in-hospital and 30-day risk-adjusted mortality (O/E); SEP-1 severe sepsis/septic shock bundle when sepsis present; guideline-concordant therapy rates (corticosteroid use in hypoxemic patients, timely remdesivir, appropriate immunomodulator use, VTE prophylaxis rate); antimicrobial stewardship / avoidance of unnecessary antibiotics (procalcitonin-guided); IMM-2 influenza plus pneumococcal and COVID vaccination at discharge; ICU utilization and mechanical ventilation rate; glycemic control on steroids; awake-proning adherence and time-to-therapy; hospital-onset infection rates (CLABSI/CAUTI)
common_complications: Progressive hypoxemic respiratory failure/ARDS requiring mechanical ventilation (mitigated by early steroids/immunomodulators, awake proning, oximetry-guided escalation); VTE/PE from hypercoagulability (risk-appropriate anticoagulation, D-dimer surveillance); AKI (avoid nephrotoxins, hemodynamic support); secondary bacterial/fungal infection including COVID-associated pulmonary aspergillosis-CAPA (stewardship, targeted workup); steroid-induced hyperglycemia (insulin protocol); delirium and deconditioning (early mobilization, minimize sedation); myocardial injury/arrhythmia; barotrauma/pneumothorax in ventilated patients
readmission_drivers: Recurrent/persistent hypoxemia after premature O2 weaning (prevent with oximetry-confirmed stability >=24h and home-O2 program); post-discharge VTE/PE (risk-stratified prophylaxis and education); secondary infection and steroid complications such as hyperglycemia/adrenal suppression (medication reconciliation and follow-up labs); deconditioning/functional decline with inadequate home support (PT/rehab and care management); long-COVID symptoms with inadequate follow-up (early PCP/pulmonary/post-COVID clinic and clear return precautions)
guideline_source: NIH COVID-19 Treatment Guidelines, COVID-19 Treatment Guidelines Panel (final version Feb 2024; authoritative standard across the 2021-2024 period); IDSA Guidelines on the Treatment and Management of Patients with COVID-19 (living guideline, updates through 2024-2025 including baricitinib vs tocilizumab); WHO Therapeutics and COVID-19: Living Guideline (2023-2024); SCCM Surviving Sepsis Campaign: COVID-19 (critical-care adjunct)
key_citations: RECOVERY Collaborative Group, Dexamethasone in Hospitalized Patients with Covid-19, NEJM 2021;384:693-704, PMID 32678530; Beigel JH et al (ACTT-1), Remdesivir for the Treatment of Covid-19 Final Report, NEJM 2020;383:1813-26, PMID 32445440; RECOVERY Collaborative Group, Tocilizumab in patients admitted to hospital with COVID-19, Lancet 2021;397:1637-45, PMID 33933206; Kalil AC et al (ACTT-2), Baricitinib plus Remdesivir for Hospitalized Adults with Covid-19, NEJM 2021;384:795-807, PMID 33306283; REMAP-CAP Investigators, IL-6 Receptor Antagonists in Critically Ill Patients with Covid-19, NEJM 2021;384:1491-1502, PMID 33631065; ATTACC/ACTIV-4a/REMAP-CAP Investigators, Therapeutic Anticoagulation with Heparin in Noncritically Ill Patients with Covid-19, NEJM 2021;385:790-802, PMID 34351721; Knight SR et al, ISARIC 4C Mortality Score, BMJ 2020;370:m3339, PMID 32907855; Ehrmann S et al, Awake prone positioning for COVID-19 acute hypoxaemic respiratory failure meta-trial, Lancet Respir Med 2021;9:1387-95, PMID 34425070
evidence_grade: Strong - multiple Class I RCT-backed recommendations anchoring current NIH/IDSA/WHO guidelines (dexamethasone, IL-6/JAK immunomodulators, remdesivir, severity-specific anticoagulation) with high-certainty mortality benefit for corticosteroid plus immunomodulator in hypoxemic patients; risk stratification (ISARIC 4C) externally validated/moderate; some operational elements (specific LOS targets, oximetry-triggered home-O2 discharge program) are consensus/extrapolated and graded lower
severity_cc_mcc_notes: Collapsed DRG 177/178/179 = respiratory infections/inflammations split by MCC/CC/none. COVID frequently carries an MCC via acute respiratory failure (J96.0x), ARDS, sepsis, AKI or acute cor pulmonale, mapping to DRG 177 with the longest LOS (~6d GMLOS), higher ICU/ventilation use, immunomodulator need and mortality (4C high/very-high). A CC-level secondary diagnosis maps to DRG 178 (~5d); moderate hypoxemic COVID without CC/MCC maps to DRG 179 (~3.7d, floor care, steroid + antiviral, faster wean). Escalation to HFNC/NIV/ventilation, shock or MODS shifts the patient into the critical-illness tier (ICU pathway, prophylactic-dose anticoagulation, LOS 10-14+d) and usually MCC coding; accurate documentation of acute respiratory failure/ARDS/sepsis is the key severity-capture lever
pathway_pearls: 1) Steroid gating - give dexamethasone 6 mg ONLY once the patient is hypoxemic/on O2 (mortality benefit in that group, potential harm without O2); do not start early and do not omit in the hypoxemic. 2) Match therapy to the oxygen tier - remdesivir earlier/lower-acuity, ADD an immunomodulator (tocilizumab or baricitinib) for rapidly escalating O2 or CRP>=75; do not wait until intubation. 3) O2 target 92-96% with oximetry-guided escalation, early awake proning and explicit NEWS2/P-F triggers prevents crash intubations and right-sizes ICU use. 4) Anticoagulation is severity-specific - therapeutic-dose in non-critically-ill (benefit) but prophylactic-dose in ICU (therapeutic dosing is harmful); an oximetry-triggered home-O2 discharge pathway safely shortens LOS
verification_notes: Independent adversarial verification pass was interrupted by a session usage limit; research is society-guideline-sourced but not yet re-checked.
scope_and_volume_notes: 2021-specific peak (Delta/Omicron); a leading cause of admission that year. Declined to ~400-500K by 2023. Severe cases -> Critical Care. Volatile year-to-year; retain for 2021-2022 but expect large decline.
drg_code_titles_v43_1: 177 — Respiratory Infections and Inflammations with MCC; 178 — Respiratory Infections and Inflammations with CC; 179 — Respiratory Infections and Inflammations without CC/MCC
volume_data_vintage: Primarily HCUP NIS 2021 national estimates; obstetric/newborn estimates cross-checked to CDC 2022 natality. Volumes were not re-estimated during the FY2026 code migration.
citation_audit_status: Completed 2026-07-21: 8/8 cited PMID references resolved in PubMed; CMS code existence and MDC/type metadata validated against v43.1; structured checks passed for risk thresholds, time targets, management detail, LOS, quality metrics, guideline dates, and citations.
clinical_verification_basis: Current pass completed citation-resolvability, coding, taxonomy, schema, and consistency checks. The source workflow contains no completed adversarial high-stakes clinical verdict for this row; clinician revalidation remains required before protocol or order-set use.
data_quality_notes: Condition-level pathways may intentionally share DRGs; this is not a one-row-per-DRG crosswalk. Current DRG overlap with pathway rank(s): 67.

admission_criteria: Cesarean delivery is itself an inpatient surgical admission — never ED-discharge or observation. Scheduled/planned CD indications: prior classical/T-incision or myomectomy, ≥2 prior CD or maternal request after counseling, placenta previa/accreta spectrum, malpresentation/breech, active genital HSV, obstructive mass, certain maternal-fetal conditions; perform elective CD at ≥39+0 wks unless medically indicated (Joint Commission PC-01). Unscheduled/intrapartum CD: arrest of dilation/descent (labor dystocia), category III / persistently non-reassuring fetal heart tracing, umbilical cord prolapse, uterine rupture, placental abruption, failed operative vaginal delivery, failed TOLAC. Emergent ('crash') CD for cord prolapse/category III/rupture. Newborns' and Mothers' Health Protection Act mandates coverage of ≥96h post-CD (a coverage floor, not a discharge target).
risk_stratification: PPH risk: CMQCC/AWHONN tool (low/medium/high) → medium=type&screen, high (previa/accreta, prior PPH, >4 births, active bleeding, coagulopathy)=type&crossmatch + hemorrhage-cart/OR planning. VTE risk: ACOG Practice Bulletin 196 risk-factor approach / Caprini or RCOG Green-top 37a score → ALL CD get mechanical prophylaxis; add pharmacologic LMWH if BMI≥40, prior VTE, thrombophilia, or immobility. Bateman Obstetric Comorbidity Index → predicts severe maternal morbidity, triages monitoring level. Preeclampsia severity (ACOG severe features: BP≥160/110, plt<100k, Cr>1.1, AST/ALT 2x ULN, pulmonary edema, cerebral/visual sx) → magnesium seizure prophylaxis + acute antihypertensives. Maternal Early Warning Criteria/Trigger (MEWC/MEWT) → rapid-response escalation. EPDS ≥10-13 → postpartum depression referral. ASA class + Mallampati airway for anesthesia planning.
initial_workup_labs: CBC with baseline Hgb/Hct and platelet count (Hgb<10-11 → iron optimization, anticipate transfusion; plt<70-100k → neuraxial-anesthesia risk assessment); ABO/Rh type & antibody screen (type & crossmatch if high PPH risk); coagulation panel (fibrinogen, PT/PTT) only if abruption, preeclampsia, active bleeding, or known coagulopathy. Preeclampsia/HELLP labs when hypertensive: CBC, creatinine, AST/ALT, LDH, uric acid, urine protein:creatinine ratio. Group B Strep status; point-of-care glucose in diabetics. Repeat postpartum Hgb if quantified blood loss high or symptomatic.
initial_imaging_dx: Obstetric ultrasound to confirm fetal presentation and placental location/previa. Suspected placenta accreta spectrum: ultrasound ± MRI for depth of invasion, deliver at accreta center of excellence with multidisciplinary plan. Continuous electronic fetal monitoring (NICHD category I-III) for all laboring/unscheduled CD to time delivery. No routine advanced imaging for uncomplicated scheduled CD. Point-of-care ultrasound (gastric/volume/cardiac) as adjunct for anesthesia in high-risk patients.
time_critical_interventions: Prophylactic antibiotics within 60 min BEFORE skin incision (SCIP/ERAS standard). Emergent CD (category III tracing, cord prolapse, uterine rupture, abruption): decision-to-incision ≤30 min target. Aspiration prophylaxis (non-particulate antacid ± H2 blocker) if general anesthesia. Delayed cord clamping 30-60s for vigorous term/preterm neonate. Skin-to-skin within minutes and breastfeeding initiation within the first hour. PPH: immediate uterotonics + quantitative blood loss; tranexamic acid 1g IV as soon as possible and within 3h of bleeding onset (WOMAN trial — earlier=greater mortality benefit); activate massive transfusion protocol for ongoing hemorrhage.
initial_management: Antibiotic prophylaxis: cefazolin 2g IV (3g if ≥120kg), redose if >1500mL blood loss or case >4h; ADD azithromycin 500mg IV for non-elective/laboring/ruptured-membrane CD (C/SOAP trial halved SSI/endometritis); clindamycin+gentamicin for severe beta-lactam allergy. Chlorhexidine-alcohol skin prep + preop vaginal prep (povidone-iodine/chlorhexidine). Neuraxial anesthesia (spinal/CSE) preferred over GA, with intrathecal morphine 50-150 mcg + multimodal analgesia (scheduled acetaminophen 1g q6h + NSAID/ketorolac), opioids as rescue only. Prophylactic phenylephrine infusion + co-load to prevent spinal hypotension; forced-air warming + warmed fluids for normothermia; maintain euvolemia. Uterotonics: oxytocin first-line (low-dose 'rule of threes' or infusion; carbetocin alternative), second-line methylergonovine 0.2mg IM (avoid in HTN), carboprost 250mcg IM (avoid in asthma), misoprostol 600-1000mcg. TXA 1g IV for PPH treatment (NOT routine prophylaxis — TRAAP2 showed no clinical benefit). Antiemetics: dexamethasone + ondansetron. Restrictive transfusion; MTP 1:1:1 for hemorrhage. Delayed cord clamping.
day1_milestones: POD0 (day of surgery): neuraxial anesthesia with intrathecal morphine delivered; immediate skin-to-skin and breastfeeding within 1h; multimodal opioid-sparing analgesia started (scheduled acetaminophen + NSAID); early oral intake — clear liquids advancing to regular diet within 2h; early mobilization out of bed within 6-8h; remove urinary catheter within 6-12h of surgery; intermittent pneumatic compression (SCDs) on; structured postpartum monitoring (fundal checks, quantified lochia, vitals per protocol) for PPH; glucose control; antiemetic prophylaxis; chewing gum to hasten bowel recovery.
day2_milestones: POD1: transition fully to oral analgesia (scheduled acetaminophen + NSAID, minimal/rescue opioids only); independent ambulation; regular diet tolerated; Foley out if not already; assess for PPH/anemia, endometritis/SSI, and VTE; lactation consult and breastfeeding support; postpartum depression screen (EPDS); contraception counseling (including immediate postpartum LARC if desired); Rho(D) immune globulin if Rh-negative and indicated; Tdap/rubella/other immunizations if due; Hgb check if hemorrhage/symptomatic; begin structured discharge teaching; consider POD1 discharge (24-48h) in uncomplicated patients on an early-discharge protocol.
day3plus_milestones: POD2+: majority discharge on POD2 once criteria met — pain controlled on oral meds, ambulating independently, voiding without retention, tolerating regular diet, afebrile, uterus firm with normal lochia, incision clean/dry/intact, Hgb acceptable/asymptomatic. Final discharge teaching and confirmed follow-up. Higher-severity (CC/MCC) tiers extend to POD3-6: severe preeclampsia on magnesium (continue seizure prophylaxis ~24h postpartum, BP control), major PPH/transfusion recovery, wound infection/endometritis on IV antibiotics, or peripartum hysterectomy.
consults_multidisciplinary: Anesthesiology (neuraxial + analgesia plan, difficult-airway/high-risk); OB anesthesia + MFM + interventional radiology + blood bank for placenta accreta spectrum; MFM for preeclampsia/complex maternal disease; lactation consultant (POD0-1); clinical pharmacy (multimodal analgesia, anticoagulation, medication reconciliation); social work/case management (psychosocial, safe discharge, substance use, housing); nutrition for diabetics; neonatology/pediatrics present at delivery; physical therapy if mobility limited; hematology for coagulopathy/thrombophilia; behavioral health for positive depression screen; acute pain service for refractory pain.
monitoring_level: Routine mother-baby/postpartum floor with standardized postpartum vital-sign + fundal-check protocol; Maternal Early Warning Criteria trigger rapid response. Enhanced/step-down monitoring for severe preeclampsia on magnesium sulfate (continuous monitoring, hourly neuro checks/reflexes/respirations/urine output), major PPH, or significant cardiac disease. ICU for eclampsia, massive transfusion/DIC, pulmonary edema, sepsis/septic shock, respiratory failure, or vasopressor need. Escalation triggers: SBP ≥160 or ≤90, HR ≥120 or <50, RR ≥30 or <10, SpO2 <95%, oliguria <35 mL/h, or altered mental status.
nutrition_mobility_vte: Nutrition: early oral intake — clear liquids to regular diet within 2h post-op; sham feeding (chewing gum) to speed return of bowel function and prevent ileus. Mobility: out of bed within 6-8h, ambulate POD0, full independent ambulation POD1. VTE: intermittent pneumatic compression for ALL CD patients placed before/continued after surgery; pharmacologic prophylaxis (enoxaparin 40mg SC daily or UFH) reserved for high-risk (prior VTE, thrombophilia, BMI ≥40, prolonged immobility) per ACOG risk stratification — routine heparin NOT recommended (ERAS). Glucose: target <180-200 mg/dL with tight capillary control, especially diabetics. Lines/catheters: remove urinary catheter within 6-12h; discontinue IV once tolerating oral intake and analgesia.
discharge_criteria: Afebrile with stable vital signs; pain controlled on oral analgesics; ambulating independently; tolerating regular diet; spontaneous voiding without retention after catheter removal; uterus firm with appropriate lochia and no active bleeding; incision clean/dry/intact without signs of infection; hemoglobin acceptable or asymptomatic if bled; no clinical signs of VTE; blood pressure controlled (especially hypertensive disorders); newborn feeding established; psychosocial and safe-discharge needs addressed.
discharge_planning: Disposition home (rare higher level of care). Medication reconciliation: scheduled acetaminophen + NSAID, limited/short opioid course with stewardship, iron if anemic, stool softener, continue antihypertensives/anticoagulation as indicated. Follow-up: BP check within 3-7 days for any hypertensive disorder; incision/wound check ~1-2 wks; comprehensive postpartum ('fourth-trimester') visit within 3 weeks and completed by 12 weeks (ACOG). Patient education on warning signs (fever, heavy/return bleeding, wound redness/discharge, unilateral leg swelling, chest pain/dyspnea, severe headache/visual changes, mood symptoms), incision care, lifting/activity limits, breastfeeding, contraception, and safe sleep. Transitions: hand-off to OB/PCP; ensure newborn pediatric visit at 48-72h.
expected_los: GMLOS ~2.6 days for uncomplicated CD (MS-DRG 785/788, without CC/MCC); national mean postpartum cesarean stay ~3.0-3.2 days; CC/MCC tiers (783/784/786/787) extend to ~4-6+ days. Group blended expected LOS ~2.6-3.2 days.
target_los: ~2 days (48h) under ERAS/ERAC pathway; selected uncomplicated patients discharge POD1 (24-48h) on early-discharge protocols. Meta-analysis shows ERAS reduces LOS ~13.8h versus traditional care without increasing readmissions.
quality_metrics: Joint Commission Perinatal Care: PC-02 NTSV (nulliparous-term-singleton-vertex) cesarean rate — Healthy People 2030 target ≤23.6%; PC-01 elective delivery <39 wks (target 0%); PC-05 exclusive breast milk feeding; PC-06 unexpected newborn complications. Prophylactic antibiotic timing ≤60 min (SCIP-derived). Perioperative VTE prophylaxis rate. Severe maternal morbidity (SMM) rate; postpartum hemorrhage rate. ERAS/ERAC protocol adherence/compliance. 30-day readmission rate; opioid prescribing/consumption; surgical site infection/endometritis rate; exclusive breastfeeding at discharge.
common_complications: Postpartum hemorrhage (uterine atony is most common) — mitigated by PPH risk stratification, active third-stage management, quantitative blood loss, oxytocin + TXA within 3h for treatment, and hemorrhage-cart/MTP readiness. Endometritis/surgical site infection — mitigated by cefazolin within 60 min + adjunctive azithromycin, chlorhexidine skin prep, and vaginal preparation. VTE/pulmonary embolism (a leading direct cause of maternal death) — mitigated by universal mechanical + risk-based pharmacologic prophylaxis and early mobilization. Ileus/PONV — mitigated by early feeding, chewing gum, multimodal antiemetics. Urinary retention — mitigated by early catheter removal with post-void monitoring. Anesthesia complications (post-dural puncture headache, spinal hypotension) — mitigated by neuraxial technique and phenylephrine. Anemia/transfusion — restrictive transfusion + iron. Wound hematoma/dehiscence — meticulous hemostasis, subcuticular closure.
readmission_drivers: Wound infection/SSI and endometritis; VTE/pulmonary embolism; hypertensive complications and delayed postpartum preeclampsia (a leading readmission driver); secondary PPH and symptomatic anemia; uncontrolled pain; mastitis. Prevention levers: extended-spectrum antibiotic prophylaxis, wound-care education with early wound check, blood-pressure self-monitoring plus follow-up within 3-7 days for hypertensive patients, risk-based VTE prophylaxis, opioid-sparing analgesia, explicit warning-sign education, early postpartum contact/telehealth outreach, and the comprehensive postpartum visit.
guideline_source: ERAS Society Guidelines for Cesarean Delivery — Parts 1-3 (Wilson/Caughey/Macones et al., Am J Obstet Gynecol 2018-2019; Part 2 2025 update); SOAP Consensus Statement and Recommendations for Enhanced Recovery After Cesarean (ERAC) (Bollag et al., Anesth Analg 2021); ACOG guidance — Practice Bulletin 199 (antibiotic prophylaxis / use of prophylactic antibiotics in labor and delivery), Practice Bulletin 196 (Thromboembolism in Pregnancy), Practice Bulletin 183 (Postpartum Hemorrhage), Committee Opinion 736 (Optimizing Postpartum Care).

evidence_grade: Strong — Class I society guidelines. Multiple current, GRADE-based society documents converge (ERAS Society Cesarean Parts 1-3 + 2025 update; SOAP ERAC consensus). Core elements are RCT-backed: antibiotic prophylaxis timing/adjunctive azithromycin (C/SOAP, NEJM), TXA for PPH treatment (WOMAN, Lancet), neuraxial + intrathecal morphine, multimodal opioid-sparing analgesia, early feeding/mobilization, delayed cord clamping. Bundle-level LOS/readmission outcomes rest on observational and meta-analytic data (moderate). Overall strength: Strong.
severity_cc_mcc_notes: Collapsed group 783-788 splits on sterilization (783-785 = with sterilization) vs none (786-788) and on severity (MCC/CC/none). Without CC/MCC (785/788): uncomplicated, floor care, GMLOS ~2.6d, ERAS target ~2d. With CC (784/787): e.g., postpartum anemia requiring transfusion, gestational/mild-moderate preeclampsia, wound infection, controlled diabetes → +0.5-1.5 days and enhanced monitoring. With MCC (783/786): severe preeclampsia/eclampsia, sepsis, major PPH with DIC/massive transfusion, peripartum hysterectomy, pulmonary edema, acute kidney injury → ICU/step-down, LOS ~4-7+ days, markedly higher severe maternal morbidity. Adding sterilization (tubal ligation) minimally changes LOS. Rising severity shifts monitoring level up, prolongs pharmacologic VTE prophylaxis, and accounts for most in-stay morbidity and readmissions.
pathway_pearls: 1) Time-critical antibiotics: cefazolin (weight-dosed 2g, 3g if ≥120kg) within 60 min pre-incision PLUS adjunctive azithromycin 500mg IV for any labored/ruptured-membrane CD — nearly halves SSI/endometritis. 2) Opioid-sparing multimodal analgesia anchored on intrathecal morphine + scheduled acetaminophen/NSAID (add TAP block only if no neuraxial morphine) — the lever that unlocks early mobilization, less ileus, and shorter LOS. 3) Standardized PPH readiness: risk stratification + quantitative blood loss + early oxytocin and TXA within 3h + hemorrhage cart/MTP. 4) Front-load recovery and close the loop: feed <2h, catheter out <12h, mobilize <8h, SCDs for all (LMWH high-risk only), then early postpartum BP/wound follow-up within 3-7 days to prevent the top readmission drivers.

No fabricated or misattributed citations. GUIDELINE_SOURCE — ACOG PB199 (Prophylactic Antibiotics in L&D, PMID 30134425), PB196 (Thromboembolism in Pregnancy, 29939938), PB183 (Postpartum Hemorrhage, 28937571), CO736 (Optimizing Postpartum Care, 30134408) all real with correct numbers/topics. QUALITY_METRICS — Joint Commission v2026B confirms PC-01 Elective Delivery, PC-02 Cesarean Birth (NTSV), PC-05 Exclusive Human Milk Feeding, PC-06 Unexpected Complications in Term Newborns; Healthy People 2030 low-risk cesarean target confirmed at 23.6% (MICH-06).
